name: Fennel and Yogurt Sauce
recipeIngredient: olive oil spray -- or 2 tablespoons oil
3/4 cup chicken broth, fresh or canned
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a small saucepan over medium-high heat; spray with olive oil or add 2 tablespoons of oil and heat the oil. Add fennel, shallots, and garlic. Cook, stirring until wilted, about 5 minutes.
2. Add the vinegar, stir, and cook for 1 minute. Add the chicken broth, Tabasco sauce, coriander, and thyme. Reduce heat and simmer for about 15 minutes.
3. Puree with a food processor until it resembles applesauce. Add the yogurt and blend again for 5 seconds. Season with freshly ground black pepper. Serve warm; if reheated, ensure not to boil.
MAKE AHEAD and keep warm. Substitute your favorite hot pepper sauce. Use low-fat yogurt.
Menu for Four: Salmon (7), Fennel and Yogurt Sauce (20), Steamed Parsleyed Potatoes with Cucumbers (15) [ 357.6 CALS, 6.1 G FAT (15.2% CFF), 422 MG SODIUM ]. The book is based on the PBS series: Pierre Franey's Cooking in Europe". Recipe tested by Pat H. June 1997. MC est. 36.8 cals, 0.8g fat, 17.5% cff, 313 mg sodium.
